items.title: Tim Cook insists soaring iPhone prices have nothing to do with Trump's tariffs — so why does the Pro Max cost $2,000?
items.description: Does the math make sense to you?

items.title: Trump Announces $100K Fee for H-1B Visas. How Big Tech Companies Could Be Affected.
items.description: The Trump administration on Friday issued a proclamation adding a $100,000 fee for H-1B visas, a special type of employment visa for highly skilled workers.  Big Tech companies are major users of these types of visas.  Six companies–  Alphabet Amazon.com Apple Meta Platforms Microsoft  and  Nvidia  –used 35,000 of the 85,000 available H-1B visas in the 2025 fiscal year.

items.title: New iPhone 17 unveiled in Moscow, as pre-orders jump despite slowing economy
items.description: MOSCOW (Reuters) -Russian resellers unveiled Apple's new iPhone 17 in stores on Saturday, as major retailer Restore: reported a sharp rise in pre-ordering this year.  Strong demand for Apple smartphones offers a sign that consumer demand for popular products has yet to falter in line with a wider economic slowdown in Russia.  The iPhones' popularity in Russia has endured Apple halting sales in 2022 and suspending services like Apple Pay in the wake of Western sanctions over the invasion of Ukraine.

items.title: Dow Jones Futures: Apple, Nvidia At Key Levels; Take Profits In These Stocks Going Vertical?
items.description: The stock market is at highs as Apple breaks out, Nvidia at critical level. Micron earnings loom. Investors face a "happy dilemma."
